title: Neurological examination — right-sided weakness
candidateInstructions: You are an FY2 on the stroke ward. Mr Dennis Whitfield, a 70-year-old retired builder, was admitted 48 hours ago following sudden onset right-sided weakness. Please perform a focused upper and lower limb neurological examination and present your findings including localisation.
examinerNotes: Left MCA territory stroke. Upper motor neurone signs right side: increased tone, brisk reflexes, extensor plantar, reduced power right arm>leg, pronator drift. Candidate should localise to cortex/internal capsule and name MCA territory.

patient.name: Dennis Whitfield
patient.presentingComplaint: Right arm and leg weakness after sudden onset 2 days ago
patient.reasonForAttendance: Admitted to stroke ward 2 days ago

hpi.onset: Sudden, 2 days ago while watching TV
hpi.character: Weakness and heaviness, arm worse than leg

examination.general: Alert man, right arm resting in slight flexion, right leg extended
examination.cardiovascular: Irregularly irregular — AF. Normal heart sounds.
examination.neurological: Alert, oriented. Right upper limb: tone increased, power 2/5, brisk reflexes, pronator drift positive. Right lower limb: tone increased, power 4/5, brisk reflexes, extensor plantar. Left side normal. No cerebellar signs. Mild expressive dysphasia improved. Right facial droop mild.

investigations.name: MRI Brain (DWI)
investigations.result: Diffusion restriction in left MCA territory (frontal, parietal, internal capsule). Established cortical infarct. No haemorrhage on FLAIR.
investigations.interpretation: Left MCA territory ischaemic infarct confirmed on DWI. Explains right-sided weakness and expressive dysphasia (Broca's area involvement).

patient.emotionalState: Frustrated with disability. Trying to be positive. Worried about recovery and independence.
patient.communicationStyle: Mild expressive dysphasia — knows what he wants to say but takes time. Patient and cooperative.
hiddenConcerns: Will I be able to drive again?
Am I going to have another stroke?
doNotVolunteer: AF diagnosis — only if asked about medical conditions or heart conditions
Apixaban — only if asked about medications

primaryDiagnosis: Left MCA Territory Ischaemic Stroke with right hemiparesis

dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Tests for upper motor neurone increase in tone (spasticity/clasp-knife)
Tests shoulder abduction, elbow flexion/extension, wrist, grip, hip flexion, knee extension/flexion, ankle
Biceps, triceps, supinator, knee, ankle jerk. Identifies brisk reflexes right side
Babinski test — identifies extensor plantar right (UMN sign)
Arms outstretched with eyes closed — right arm pronates and drifts down

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: States upper motor neurone signs in right limbs

interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Patient explanation adapted for communication difficulty
Explains neurological findings in lay terms

references.source: NICE NG128: Stroke and transient ischaemic attack in over 16s

learningObjectives: Perform systematic neurological examination of limbs
